description: Postgres tenancy foundation for service state

context.summary: Replace Mongo stateManager with Postgres-backed tenancy + config state (migration-light spine + JSONB), and prepare all service features (router, dashboard, HITL) to be tenant-scoped.

subtasks.description: Choose migration tool and schema spine
tasks: Research a migration approach (raw SQL migrations, minimal tooling) aligned with 'minimize migrations'
Define baseline schema spine for: tenants, api_keys, wa_sessions, webhook_registrations, audit_log
Define JSONB/event tables for: events, messages, conversation_state
Define scoping rules: how tenantId is derived from API key and how it scopes sessionId and routes
verification: Schema plan documented in task output fields and matches Appendix A in spec/10-service-dashboard-prd.md
No more than additive schema changes required for foreseeable PRD features
docs/architecture/ planned: explain tenancy spine tables and JSONB event storage approach.
subtasks.output: Migration tooling choice + baseline schema for tenancy + event/state storage

subtasks.description: Add Postgres connection and migrations
tasks: Add Postgres config/env vars to whatsapp-service config (DATABASE_URL, pool settings)
Create baseline migrations for tenancy spine and event/state tables
Implement a small typed DB access layer (no ORM requirement)
Add API key hashing + lookup + last_used_at tracking
verification: Service boots successfully with Postgres configured and database connectivity verified.
Migrations apply cleanly on a fresh database without manual intervention steps.
API key auth still works and now resolves tenant context deterministically.
subtasks.output: Postgres-backed tenancy + auth foundation in whatsapp-service

subtasks.description: Move webhook/config state from Mongo to Postgres
tasks: Replace MongoDB webhook registration storage with Postgres webhook_registrations
Replace MongoDB config storage with Postgres config JSONB (or tenant_config table)
Remove MONGODB_URI requirement from config when Postgres is enabled
Ensure existing webhook routing task 024 can consume the new storage API
verification: Webhook register/unregister/list endpoints work against Postgres in local development environment.
No MongoDB dependency is required for core routing and dashboard features.
Backward compatibility strategy is documented if Mongo is still supported temporarily.
subtasks.output: stateManager replaced (or dual-run) with Postgres-backed implementation

changelog: Added Postgres tenancy foundation for dashboard, routing, and state

context.summary: Provide durable conversation state for stateless n8n workflows, plus a safe handoff model for external providers (ordering platforms or MCP-backed flows) with optimistic concurrency and audit logging.

subtasks.description: Implement /state read-write-reset endpoints
tasks: Implement GET/PUT/DELETE /state/:sessionId/:chatId with tenant scoping
Add optimistic concurrency via expectedStateVersion (409 on mismatch)
Define minimal state envelope (stage/owner/provider/context/external) as per PRD Appendix A
Record state changes in audit_log and events tables
verification: Concurrent updates correctly return 409 conflicts when expectedStateVersion mismatches occur.
State changes create audit log entries with actor, target, before, and after.
Endpoints are tenant-scoped and do not allow cross-tenant reads under any key.
docs/dashboard/ planned: describe conversation state in simple terms for business users.
subtasks.output: State API contract implemented and usable from n8n

subtasks.description: Implement /handoff start-advance-complete
tasks: Implement POST /handoff/:sessionId/:chatId/start to initialize external ownership and stage
Implement POST /handoff/:sessionId/:chatId/advance to update stage + patch state safely
Implement POST /handoff/:sessionId/:chatId/complete to finalize and return ownership to n8n/human
Add provider allowlists and signature verification for external callbacks (design + placeholder implementation)
verification: Stage transitions are recorded in events and audit_log with correlation identifiers.
Only allowed providers can update stage/owner, enforced by RBAC and allowlists.
Handoff endpoints enforce expectedStateVersion when provided, returning 409 on mismatch.
docs/dashboard/ planned: add an operator guide for handoff stages and ownership.
subtasks.output: External stage handoff supported for ordering/MCP flows

changelog: Added conversation state and external handoff endpoints for stateless workflows

context.summary: Make routing and UI stable by normalizing inbound event types/shapes, generating correlation IDs, and preventing duplicate forwards/sends across retries and multi-target fanout.

tasks: Implement a normalized internal event shape (dataType mapping, flatten key fields)
Generate and propagate x-correlation-id for all forwards and upstream calls
Derive stable eventId from WhatsApp message identifiers when available
Implement dedupe window (eventId + tenantId + sessionId) to prevent double-processing
Store normalized events for dashboard replay/debugging (JSONB event store)
verification: Both 'message' and 'message_create' style inputs route consistently
Duplicate inbound deliveries do not cause duplicate forwards
Correlation IDs appear in logs/events and can be searched
docs/dashboard/ planned: explain correlationId usage for business and technical users.
changelog: Improved event normalization, correlation, and deduplication for reliable routing

description: RBAC, audit log, and redaction policies

context.summary: Business ops requires RBAC and immutable audit trails. Add role/scopes and tenant-level redaction policies so logs/messages do not leak sensitive data.

tasks: Define roles + scopes model (creator_admin, tenant_admin, agent, automation_engineer, read_only)
Enforce tenant scoping and route-level authorization using API key scopes
Implement audit log entries for operator actions and sensitive API operations
Implement redaction policies applied at ingest + at display (mask raw payload when configured)
Add SSRF protections / allowlists for webhook target URLs
verification: Cross-tenant access is blocked for all APIs and admin UI reads
Audit entries are written for manual message send, takeover, and routing edits
Redaction rules are applied consistently and do not leak x-api-key or auth headers
docs/guides/user-management/ planned: explain RBAC roles and audited actions for admins.
changelog: Added RBAC, audit logging, and redaction policies for secure operations

context.summary: Add alerting for session disconnects/QR-required states, sustained routing failures, and delivery anomalies; expose in dashboard and optionally forward to external channels.

tasks: Define alert types (disconnect, qr_required, routing_failures, high_latency, duplicate_spike)
Implement alert detection using health endpoints + event stream metrics
Persist alerts per tenant and expose via API + real-time UI stream
Add configurable notification channels per tenant (webhook/email/slack - start with webhook)
Add alert acknowledgements and assignments for operators
verification: Disconnect events produce alerts visible in dashboard
Sustained n8n webhook failures produce alerts without spamming
Acknowledging an alert is audited and does not leak across tenants
docs/dashboard/ planned: add a business-user alerts guide and escalation steps.
changelog: Added alerting and notifications for session and routing incidents

context.summary: Define and implement default retention/indexing to keep Postgres performant while supporting dashboard timelines and audits.

tasks: Define retention defaults per tenant for events/messages/conversation_state (e.g. 7/30/90 day tiers)
Add required indexes for tenant/session/chat/correlation/event lookup paths
Implement periodic cleanup job (safe deletes) and document ops guidance
Add optional partitioning strategy plan if volumes require it
verification: Retention job can be run safely without breaking active conversations
Key queries remain performant with indexes present
Docs describe how to adjust retention per tenant
changelog: Added retention and indexing defaults for Postgres-backed dashboard data

description: Add integration API keys and user PATs (tenant-scoped)

context.summary: Implement tenant-scoped API key storage and management. Raw keys are never stored, only hashes; keys are revocable/rotatable; PATs attribute actions to a user. Note: task 043 (Keycloak auth) is now complete.

subtasks.description: Define scope schema and rotation rules

context.summary: Scopes must cover: allowed sessionIds, allowed actions, allowed webhook target domains, and optional redaction policies.

tasks: Define scope JSON schema for api_keys.scopes and how it maps to RBAC permissions
Define rotation rules (create new, dual-run, revoke old) and audit requirements
Define separate key types: integration (n8n) vs PAT (user) and required metadata per type
verification: Scope schema exists as a concrete JSON shape with fields, types, and defaults.
Rotation flow is explicit and includes auditing on create, rotate, and revoke.
Key type differences are documented clearly (integration keys vs user PATs).
docs/guides/user-management/ planned: explain scopes in business-friendly language and examples clearly.
subtasks.output: Scope schema + rotation rules + key type model

subtasks.description: Design key management endpoints and RBAC rules

context.prior_output: 044.1 Scope schema + rotation rules + key type model

tasks: Define endpoints: POST /api/keys (create), GET /api/keys (list), DELETE /api/keys/:id (revoke), POST /api/keys/:id/rotate
Define which roles can manage which key types (creator_admin vs tenant_admin) and which roles can create PATs
Define response payloads: key metadata never returns raw secret; creation returns raw secret exactly once
Define how last_used_at is updated without logging secrets or creating a write hotspot
verification: Key management endpoints support create/list/revoke/rotate flows required by Admin UI.
RBAC rules for key management are explicit and least-privilege by default.
Creation contract returns the raw key once and it cannot be fetched again.
docs/guides/user-management/ planned: document key rotation steps and safety warnings clearly.
subtasks.output: Key management API contract + RBAC rules

subtasks.description: Implement key create/list/revoke/rotate endpoints

context.prior_output: 044.2 Key management API contract + RBAC rules

tasks: Implement key hashing and constant-time token comparison for authentication
Implement key CRUD + rotation endpoints with tenant scoping and RBAC gates
Write audit_log entries for create/revoke/rotate with actor attribution (user vs PAT vs integration)
Update last_used_at on successful authentication (without storing or logging raw token)
verification: Creating a key returns a raw secret once and stores only a hash.
Revoked keys are rejected with 403 and do not affect other tenants.
Audit log contains entries for create/revoke/rotate with correct actor attribution metadata.
docs/guides/user-management/ planned: include key lifecycle examples for admins and operators.
subtasks.output: Key management endpoints implemented with audit logging

docs/guides/user-management/ planned: add curl examples for PAT usage and revocation.
docs/n8n/ planned: add integration key setup examples for n8n HTTP Request nodes.
Docs contain no real secrets; all examples use placeholders only.
subtasks.output: Key auth is tested and documented for operators and automation engineers

description: Implement real-time stream + observability API for dashboard

context.summary: Provide APIs and a websocket stream so the dashboard can view sessions/logs/events/messages side-by-side with filters and correlation IDs.

subtasks.description: Define stream event schema and filters

tasks: Define EventEnvelope JSON: { tenantId, sessionId, eventType, level, eventId, correlationId, ts, payload }
Define log redaction rules applied before emit/storage (mask API keys, auth headers)
Define websocket channels or subscription messages for filtering to avoid flooding clients

Implement subscription filtering by tenantId and optionally sessionId/eventType to control volume
Emit structured events for: inbound webhook received, routing decision, outbound send result, session lifecycle events, errors
Implement a circular buffer replay (last N events) for new connections
verification: Authenticated client receives events without refreshing the page in normal usage.

No endpoint can be used to read cross-tenant events/audits, even with guessing IDs.

description: Implement redaction and SSRF guardrails for dashboard and routing

context.summary: Prevent secret leakage and unsafe outbound routing in a multi-tenant environment.

subtasks.description: Enumerate redaction fields and SSRF threat model

context.summary: Define exactly what must be redacted and what outbound targets must be blocked/allowed.

tasks: Enumerate sensitive fields to redact (x-api-key, Authorization, cookies, webhook secrets, tokens) including nested JSON cases
Define tenant redaction policy options (mask message bodies, store only hashes, retain minimal metadata)
Define SSRF policy: allowed schemes, allowed domains per tenant, private IP and localhost blocking, redirect handling
verification: Redaction key list is explicit and covers headers and nested JSON payloads.
Tenant redaction policy options are concrete and map directly to PRD requirements.
SSRF policy includes redirect behavior and private-range blocking rules for safety.
docs/dashboard/ planned: describe what business users can and cannot see in logs.
subtasks.output: Redaction keys + tenant policy options + SSRF rules

subtasks.description: Implement centralized redaction utility and policy enforcement

context.summary: Implement redaction at ingest (before store) and at emit (before websocket/API responses).

tasks: Implement a single redaction function used by logging, event streaming, and API responses
Apply tenant policy at ingest: store raw payload only if permitted; otherwise store masked payload + hash
Ensure audit_log never stores secrets (store summaries/metadata only)
verification: Redaction is applied consistently across logs, websocket events, and REST APIs.
Tenant policy can mask message bodies without breaking correlationId-based search and filters.
Audit entries contain no secrets but remain useful for forensics and accountability.
docs/dashboard/ planned: add redaction policy explanation for business stakeholders and operators.

subtasks.description: Implement SSRF protections for webhook targets

context.summary: Block unsafe outbound webhook targets and enforce tenant allowlists.

tasks: Validate webhook target URLs on registration and on forward (defense in depth)
Enforce tenant allowlists for domains and block localhost/private IP ranges by default
Reject redirects to disallowed hosts and set sane timeouts on outbound calls
verification: Registering a disallowed URL fails with 400 and a clear error message.
Forwarding never occurs to disallowed targets, even if registration data is tampered.
Redirects to private IPs are rejected and logged as security violations.
docs/guides/authentication/ planned: document allowed target rules and safe defaults clearly.
subtasks.output: SSRF protections enforced on routing targets

subtasks.description: Add security tests and document guardrails

context.summary: Add regression coverage and document tenant security controls.

tasks: Add tests ensuring secrets never appear in emitted events/log payloads
Add tests ensuring SSRF targets are blocked (localhost/private ranges/redirects)
Update docs/ with redaction policy and SSRF allowlist configuration per tenant
verification: Run `npm test` and confirm security regression tests pass reliably in CI.
docs/dashboard/ planned: document redaction policies and example masked event views.
docs/guides/authentication/ planned: document SSRF allowlists, blocked ranges, and error meanings.
Regression tests prevent reintroducing secret leakage and unsafe forwarding behavior.
subtasks.output: Security guardrails are tested and documented

changelog: Added redaction policies and SSRF protections for secure multi-tenant operations
